releaseNotes: v2.10.5 — Timecode routing, LTC Manager, and stability

history.notes:
v2.10.5 — Timecode routing, LTC Manager, and stability

Major release rolling up everything since v2.8.9 (last general release).
Two big new features (LTC reception + Art-Net Timecode forwarding,
v2.9.0; LTC Manager output window, v2.10.0) and three follow-up patches
that hardened them.

═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
NEW: Timecode routing  (v2.9.0)
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

ArtNet Manager can now both receive and forward SMPTE timecode.

• Two mutually-exclusive reception sources, picked in Settings:
  · Art-Net TC (OpCode 0x9700) — already bound to the configured NIC
  · LTC audio — renderer-side AudioWorklet decoder reading from any
    selected audio input device. Self-calibrating biphase mark decoder,
    auto-adapts to 24/25/29.97/30 fps without prior configuration.

• Art-Net Timecode forwarding to multiple destinations. Each entry has
  IP/port plus an optional type override (Film/EBU/DF/SMPTE). No rate
  limiting — TC is intrinsically ≤30 Hz. Cluster STANDBY mutes TC
  forwarding alongside DMX forwarding.

• Settings → Timecode Source UI for picking source kind + LTC input
  device. The on-screen TC display gets a small "LTC" / "TC" badge
  to indicate the active source.

═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
NEW: LTC Manager  (v2.10.0, originally "LTC Output")
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

A separate window (Tools → LTC Manager, Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+L) that takes
incoming Art-Net Timecode and re-encodes it as SMPTE LTC audio out to
any selected audio device. Combined with the LTC reception path on the
sender side, this lets LTC bridge the internet via Art-Net TC and be
reconstructed at the destination.

• Mono channel routing. The encoder feeds a ChannelMergerNode whose
  output width matches the device's maxChannelCount, so the LTC signal
  goes to ONE selected physical channel and silence on every other
  channel. Multichannel audio interfaces can carry LTC alongside other
  audio on neighbouring channels. (Default: mono channel 1.)

• Free-running bit cursor. The audio thread runs an uninterrupted
  biphase cursor; sync messages from the parent thread queue the new
  TC value into pendingTc and are applied at the next 80-bit frame
  boundary. This keeps the bit stream rock-steady, which is what
  decoders need to lock on. (See Stability fixes below for the
  regression history.)

• Configurable drift handling:
  · Resync Threshold (frames) — drift below is absorbed by the queued-
    sync path; drift above forces a hard cursor reset to snap back.
    Default 4 frames.
  · On Source Loss — Mute Output (default) or Free-run. A 250 ms
    watchdog detects stall when no sync arrives within stallTimeoutMs.
  · Stall Timeout (ms) — default 1000.
  · Force Resync button for manual snap-back.

• Visual indicators on the "Output (encoded)" card:
  · Cyan / In Sync — drift within tolerance
  · Red / Drift +Nf — drift over threshold
  · Amber / Source Lost — stall detected

• Two source modes (default: Main App):
  · Main App — mirrors the timecode the main ArtNet Manager window
    already receives, via a new IPC channel. No second UDP socket. The
    LTC Manager and the main app's Art-Net reception coexist without
    fighting over port 6454.
  · Standalone UDP listener — opt-in, useful when the Manager runs on
    a host where the main app isn't receiving Art-Net at all.

═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Stability fixes  (v2.10.2 → v2.10.5)
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

• v2.10.2 — Mono routing fix.
  The original encoder connected a mono worklet straight to the audio
  destination, which Web Audio upmixed to stereo (L = R). Several LTC
  decoders treated the upmixed signal as corrupted. Now true mono via
  ChannelMergerNode with discrete channel interpretation.

• v2.10.3 — Decoder lock fix (root cause of v2.10.0 receive issues).
  The encoder reset its 80-bit cursor on every received Art-Net TC
  packet (24-30 Hz). Because biphase mark relies on perfectly periodic
  boundary transitions, that meant the bit stream was being torn
  mid-bit dozens of times per second and decoders only locked
  intermittently. The cursor is now decoupled from sync arrival —
  syncs queue, the cursor swaps in the queued TC at the natural frame
  boundary. Worst-case packet jitter produces ≤1 frame of TC drift,
  corrected at the next boundary.

• v2.10.4 — Source sharing + drift indicators.
  See "NEW: LTC Manager" above. Eliminates the port-6454 race against
  the main app's ArtNetEngine.

• v2.10.5 — Drop-frame correctness.
  · The worklet's offset-handler and the engine's applyOffset() now
    snap forward to frame 02 when DF arithmetic lands on the skipped
    frames 00/01 at non-10th-minute boundaries. Strict LTC decoders
    were rejecting those frames as SMPTE-invalid.
  · tcToFrames / framesBetween are now drop-frame aware: skipped
    frames are subtracted from the elapsed-frame count, so drift
    measured across a non-10th-minute boundary in 29.97 DF mode is no
    longer off by 2 frames.
  · LtcReceiver.stop() now clears its 'error' status field so a stale
    start failure no longer lingers when the user switches the source
    back to Art-Net.
  · LtcUdpListener comment corrected — the socket uses reuseAddr only,
    not SO_REUSEPORT; same-port coexistence is platform-dependent and
    not guaranteed (Main App TC mode is the recommended path).

═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
UX additions
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

• Drag-and-drop reordering of:
  · Device list (auto-discovered fixtures)
  · Forwarding destinations table (DMX out)
  Persists to disk via DeviceOrderStore + ConfigPersistence.

• ANMR recording compatibility classification.
  Each .anmr now carries the writer's app version (informational, in
  magic bytes 5-7) and a separate format-version byte (8) used for
  real compatibility decisions:
  · ok                  — same format version, writer ≤ current
  · unknown-app-version — same format, writer newer (⚠ badge, plays)
  · legacy-format       — older format, played via compat reader (⚠)
  · future-format       — newer format, refused with "Cannot Play"
                          and an actionable update prompt.
  Player playlist surfaces the writer version next to each file date.

═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Architecture notes
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

• New main-process modules:
  · src/main/engine/TimecodeForwarder.ts
  · src/main/discovery/DeviceOrderStore.ts
  · src/main/recording/Compatibility.ts
  · src/main/ltc-output/window.ts + LtcUdpListener.ts
• New renderer modules:
  · src/renderer/src/audio/{LtcReceiver,ltcLifecycle}.ts
  · src/renderer/src/audio/ltc-worklet.js (decoder)
  · src/renderer/ltc-output/ (LTC Manager window + encoder worklet)
  · src/renderer/src/components/forwarding/TimecodeForwardingSection.tsx
  · src/renderer/src/components/settings/TimecodeSourceSection.tsx
• New IPC channels: TIMECODE_LTC_INGEST, TIMECODE_LTC_STATUS,
  TIMECODE_FORWARD_*, LTC_OUT_*, DEVICE_ORDER_*, DESTINATION_REORDER.

═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Compatibility
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

• Existing .anmr recordings produced by v2.8.9 and earlier remain
  fully playable — readers ignore the writer-version magic bytes and
  classify by ANMR_FORMAT_VERSION (still 1).
• Settings file gains new fields (timecodeSource, ltcAudioDeviceId,
  timecode forwarding destinations, device-order list). Older settings
  files load with sane defaults.

41 files changed, +4260 / -55 across 11 commits.

ANMR: classify recording compatibility + show writer version in Playlist

Define and implement the project's ANMR compatibility policy end-to-end.

Reader side (main process):
- readFileHeader() now extracts the writer's app version from magic bytes
  5-7 into RecordingMetadata.writerVersion (still informational — the
  identity check still uses only the 5-byte prefix).
- New src/main/recording/Compatibility.ts exposes classifyCompatibility()
  which returns one of:
    - 'ok'                   format match, writer ≤ current → play normally
    - 'unknown-app-version'  format match, writer > current → play with ⚠️
    - 'legacy-format'        format < current               → legacy compat ⚠️
    - 'future-format'        format > current               → cannot play
- PlaybackEngine.load() refuses 'future-format' files with a clear error.
- RecordingFileScanner populates writerVersion / formatVersion /
  compatibility on every PlaylistEntry.

UI side (renderer):
- Playlist row displays the recorded writer version () next to
  the file date so operators can see which build produced each file.
- Non-'ok' compatibility shows an amber ⚠️ badge (UnknownVersion /
  LegacyFormat) with a tooltip explaining the implication.
- 'future-format' entries are greyed out, line-through, Ban icon, and
  both single- and double-click are disabled — they cannot be loaded.

Docs:
- CLAUDE.md gains a Compatibility Rules table that codifies the verdicts
  and the forward-looking policy: future ANMR_FORMAT_VERSION bumps must
  ship a legacy reader for the previous format, and the legacy badge is
  the user-visible signal that some newer features may be unreproducible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2.8.9 - macOS code signing & notarization

The macOS build is now signed with a Developer ID certificate and
notarized by Apple. The 'unidentified developer' Gatekeeper warning
no longer appears when opening the app for the first time.
Notification system (toast popups, operator announcements, critical alerts), cluster detach/rejoin UX, reset dismissed announcements, bug fixes
Fix: app failed to launch after Permanent Shutdown — bootstrap now clears sentinel file on fresh launch, preventing guardian deadlock

Fix: recording save dir overwritten by settings save. Feat: player file-not-found red banner + auto-refresh, save dir display with open-in-file-manager button. AppSettings disk persistence. Defensive validation for NIC/sACN.

Signal recording/playback, SignalFlow performance (batch draw, simplified mode), cluster promote with split-brain prevention, About/version check, diagnostic snapshot for bug reports, enriched startup logs, documentation update
About dialog, version check, bug report & feature request
Interactive tooltips, split-brain prevention on double promote
Manual Promote to PRIMARY button for cluster
Reduce GC pressure and allocations on frame hot path
Integrate Signal Checker as built-in mini-app
Reliability improvements, IPC race condition fix
Merge engine, multi-NIC, display formats, guardian mode
